business_problem: Conduent—which processes nearly 13 million roadway tolling transactions every day and billions of customer-service interactions per year for 6 of the 10 largest US toll systems—needed faster transaction performance for one large US tolling client whose Microsoft-based front-end and Oracle Database back-end on premises could not keep up.
solution_summary: Conduent, a global digital services provider processing 13M tolling transactions daily across 6 major US toll systems, migrated a high-volume tolling customer's back-end database workload from on-premises Oracle Database to Oracle Database@Azure—co-locating OCI services inside Azure data centers. This architecture enabled customers to keep their Microsoft front-end stack (Dynamics 365, Azure) while Conduent provided Exadata performance on the back end, doubling application performance, reducing transaction latency, and maintaining Microsoft/Oracle licensing flexibility.
business_outcome: Conduent doubled tolling-application performance versus on-premises, improved system resiliency, met regulatory and Microsoft-licensing requirements via Azure co-location, and positioned its data for future AI/ML use cases across European and Australian transit expansion.
metrics: ~13 million roadway tolling transactions per day
Supports 6 of the 10 largest US toll systems
Double the application performance vs on-premises
architecture_clues: Oracle Database@Azure: OCI Exadata services co-located inside Azure data centers
Microsoft Dynamics 365 front end integrated with Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP
Distributed cloud architecture spanning Oracle Database@Azure, Exadata Cloud@Customer, and OCI public regions under one control plane
Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitments (MACC) used alongside Oracle licensing
evidence_quotes: Oracle Database@Azure helped Conduent double the application performance compared with an on-premises deployment and improve system resiliency without impacting regulatory compliance.
Conduent processes nearly 13 million roadway tolling transactions every day, and billions of customer service interactions a year.
The performance of Exadata Database Service on Oracle Database@Azure is amazing—better than anticipated.

solution_components.name: Oracle Database@Azure
solution_components.role: High-performance transactional database for tolling and payment processing co-located in Azure data centers; doubles performance vs. on-premises and provides real-time toll account updates.

solution_components.name: Oracle Exadata Database Service
solution_components.role: Optimized database engine delivering sub-millisecond query performance for 13M daily toll transactions and billing operations.

solution_components.name: Microsoft Dynamics 365
solution_components.role: Front-end toll account management and customer-facing interface; customers replenish toll accounts through integrated portal.

solution_components.name: Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP
solution_components.role: Back-office financial transaction recording and reconciliation of toll revenue.

data_flow: Toll collection transactions from toll plazas and readers → Oracle Exadata Database on Azure → real-time account updates via Dynamics 365 front-end → Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP for financial posting; contact data stored in Dynamics 365.
